findings.title: --no-role-prompts=false silently overrides ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true
findings.summary: RolePrompts: rolePrompts && !noRolePrompts combined with RolePromptsSet = Changed("role-prompts") || Changed("no-role-prompts") allows --no-role-prompts=false to mark the flag as set and force RolePrompts=false at the highest precedence, violating the flag/env precedence contract.
messages: CLI flag composition `RolePrompts: rolePrompts && !noRolePrompts` combined with `RolePromptsSet = Changed("role-prompts") || Changed("no-role-prompts")` lets `--no-role-prompts=false` (or any explicit `=false`) silently override `ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true`

findings.title: AutoPhaseDiffPrompt subset directive misapplied to single-phase runs
findings.summary: resolvePrompts (internal/agent/diff_review.go:30) routes any non-"arch" Phase to AutoPhaseDiffPrompt, which states the reviewer sees only a subset and that an arch reviewer has the full diff. In auto-phase small / flat medium fallback the reviewer actually sees the full diff and there is no arch reviewer, so cross-file findings are suppressed by a misleading directive.
messages: `resolvePrompts` default branch applies `AutoPhaseDiffPrompt` ("You are reviewing a subset of files from a larger change") to any non-"arch" Phase value, including small/single-phase auto-phase runs where the reviewer actually sees the full diff

internal/agent/diff_review.go:30: `RolePrompts` is applied to any non-empty phase, so auto-phase small or flat medium fallback reviewers get `AutoPhaseDiffPrompt` even though they receive the full diff and no subset/arch split exists

findings.title: Ref-file prompts dropped explicit "Read this file" instruction
findings.summary: AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt / AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt no longer carry the explicit read-the-file directive that distinguished ref-file mode from inline-diff mode, so agents may not realize they must open %s instead of expecting inline diff content. Cross-check escalated this to blocking because the regression spans prompts and diff_review consumers.
messages: `AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt` / `AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt` drop the explicit "Read this file" instruction that distinguishes ref-file mode from inline-diff mode; agents may not realize they need to open `%s` rather than expect inline content

info.title: Reviewer process notes (no actionable finding)
info.summary: Reviewers 2, 3, and 4 recorded methodology notes — checking memory/context, verifying that the pasted diff matches current files, tracing config-resolution and runner spec paths, and scoping the review to subset-files/code-level only — without raising independent issues.
messages: ACR_win の既存経緯が関係しうるので、まず最小限だけメモと該当コードを確認してから、diff 内で言える実害だけに絞って返します。
現行コード上では `RolePrompts` は `ReviewConfig` 側で `Phase != ""` と合わせて解釈される実装でした。runner の呼び出し境界と spec 生成も読んで確認します。
「auto-phase 用」と書かれたフラグが、`Phase` を持つ他の実行経路にも乗るかどうかを grouped diff の spec とプロンプト解決まで追って確認します。
I'll review this as a read-only code review and ground any finding in the actual files, not just the pasted diff. Doing a quick memory pass for prior ACR workflow context.

The main surface here is config resolution plus prompt selection. Checking how the new flag reaches each reviewer implementation, because a bug can hide if one agent path bypasses the shared diff-review helper.
関連するレビュー運用メモと実ファイル側の該当箇所を確認して、diff だけでなく現在のコード上の行番号で判断します。
code-review skill のチェックリストを使うが、subset files / code-level / 指定フォーマット に絞る。workspace の `git diff` は空だったため、貼られた diff と実ファイルの現状を照合。
貼付 diff の変更自体は workspace には未適用だが、同じ変更内容が現行ファイルには既に入っている。`resolvePrompts` の呼び出し経路と config 解決順序を確認中。
現行実装では `Phase` は runner specs から `"arch"`/`"diff"` として渡り、追加された config 値も runner まで伝搬する。ref-file モード時に新プロンプトへ差し替わる箇所を確認中。

findings.title: Codex reviewer path was not covered by any diff group
findings.summary: The arch review reports that the Codex reviewer bypasses the shared prompt-resolution path, but no succeeding diff group includes internal/agent/codex.go, so the agent-specific failure mode was only seen by the full-diff arch lane and remains a grouped-review coverage gap.

findings.title: Flag precedence bug spans CLI and config/test boundaries
findings.summary: The arch review reports an explicit false flag overriding ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true. That behavior crosses cmd/acr flag-state construction and internal/config resolution/tests, but no diff finding covers the combined precedence path across those groups.

findings.title: Misleading diff prompt is confirmed across arch and diff review
findings.summary: Both the full-diff arch lane and a subset diff lane identify that non-arch phases can receive the subset-oriented AutoPhaseDiffPrompt. Because this prompt-selection issue affects full-diff auto-phase executions outside the specific reviewed subset, the multi-group context confirms it as broader than a local prompt wording issue.

findings.title: Codex agent bypasses RolePrompts plumbing — feature is silently agent-dependent
findings.summary: The arch reviewer flagged that codex.go builds its own command and never routes through executeDiffBasedReview/resolvePrompts. None of the diff groups (g01 covering codex.go's caller surface, g02 covering prompts/config, g03 covering runner) detected this because each group's slice does not include internal/agent/codex.go itself; the gap is that the file responsible for the silent no-op was outside every diff group's target_files. This is a coverage gap that only surfaces in the cross-group view: the per-group reviewers cannot confirm or refute the claim against the actual codex.go contents.

findings.title: AutoPhaseDiffPrompt subset directive applied to single-phase runs — cross-file findings suppressed
findings.summary: Both the arch reviewer (id 2) and g01's diff reviewer (id 13) independently identified that resolvePrompts applies AutoPhaseDiffPrompt to any non-arch Phase value, including auto-phase small/flat runs that see the full diff. The prompt falsely tells reviewers they are reviewing a subset with an arch reviewer covering the rest. Because small/flat phases create only diff reviewers (no arch reviewer exists in those phases), cross-file architectural findings have no reviewer at all — this is a structural cross-group coverage gap, not just a prompt wording issue. Escalating g01's advisory severity to blocking based on the arch reviewer's confirmation and the structural impact.

findings.title: CLI flag precedence violation spans config and CLI layers
findings.summary: Arch finding id 1 identifies that --no-role-prompts=false silently overrides ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true via the RolePromptsSet computation. The CLI composition lives in g01's slice (cmd/acr/main.go, cmd/acr/review.go) while the precedence resolution lives in g02's slice (internal/config/config.go) and g03's slice (internal/config/config_test.go). Neither g01, g02, nor g03 caught this in isolation because the bug emerges only from the interaction of CLI composition and config-layer Set-flag tracking — a classic cross-boundary defect that was only visible to the arch reviewer with full-diff context.

findings.title: Ref-file prompt regression spans prompts and diff_review consumers
findings.summary: Arch finding id 4 (advisory) reports that AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt/AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt drop the explicit 'Read this file' instruction. The prompt definitions live in g02 (internal/agent/prompts.go) but the consumer that selects ref-file mode lives in g01 (internal/agent/diff_review.go). Per upstream cross-check context this issue was escalated to blocking because agents may run without actual diff content. Neither g01 nor g02 in isolation could observe the regression: g01 sees the selection logic but not the prompt body; g02 sees the prompt body but not how the selection compares to the prior per-agent prompts.

findings.title: Config Precedence Bug Missed by Diff Group
findings.summary: The arch group identified a blocking flag composition bug where `--no-role-prompts=false` silently overrides `ACR_ROLE_PROMPTS=true` (id 1). Group g02 reviewed `internal/config/config.go` but failed to report this code-level issue.

findings.title: Prompt Instruction Regression Missed by Diff Group
findings.summary: The arch group found that `AutoPhaseDiffRefFilePrompt` and `AutoPhaseArchRefFilePrompt` drop the explicit 'Read this file' instruction (id 4). Group g02 reviewed `internal/agent/prompts.go` but did not catch this regression.

findings.title: Subset Prompt Incorrectly Applied to Full Diff
findings.summary: Both the arch group (id 2) and diff group g01 (id 13) independently identified that `resolvePrompts` incorrectly applies the subset prompt to full-diff reviewers when the phase is not 'arch', which will falsely tell agents they are only reviewing a subset of files. This corroborates a critical logic flaw.

findings.title: Codex Agent Bypass Unaddressed in Code Changes
findings.summary: The arch group noted that the Codex agent entirely bypasses the new `RolePrompts` logic because it builds its own command (id 0). This cross-file architectural issue is a gap as it is not addressed or fixed in any of the modified files reviewed by the diff groups.
